page: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v3 is a virtual machine in the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. This instance does not include local storage or a GPU, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks reflect its single-core configuration. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core score both rank in the average tier, with Geekbench scoring 1628.0. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as the stress-ng multi-core test and Geekbench multi-core score of 1640.0, fall into the lower tier due to the single-vCPU limitation. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds recorded at 13.2 GB/sec and 10.83 GB/sec, respectively. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform moderately, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 28.27 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC1s_v3 offers a low-cost entry point to dedicated Intel Xeon resources with a relatively high memory allocation per core. However, the lack of local storage and low multi-core processing power present clear tradeoffs for compute-heavy tasks. This server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic database operations, small-scale static web serving, or utility workloads that require a dedicated CPU core and a moderate memory footprint without intensive parallel processing requirements.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC1s_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor clocked at 2.8 GHz, it features 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1628.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 28.27 GB/sec. However, its single-core architecture limits multi-core and parallel processing capabilities, as reflected in lower multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks. Qualitatively, it serves as a cost-efficient option for dedicated, memory-dense single-core workloads. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale utility tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual server featuring a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual machine with a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for light single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A dedicated single-core virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 1 vCPU and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No local storage or GPU, with support for Premium Storage
Average single-core performance for basic computing and compression tasks
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and utility workloads
